sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἔμπροσθεν

- Transliteration: Emprosthen

- Phonetic: em'-pros-then

- Definition:

1. in front, before

a. before, i.e. in that local region which is in front of a person or thing

b. before, in the presence of, i.e. opposite to, over against one

c. before, in the sight of

d. before, denoting rank

- Origin: from G1722 and G4314

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adverb

- Strong's: From G1722 and G4314; in front of (in place [literally or figuratively] or time): - against at before (in presence sight) of.
